COLUMN: A massive surge in US exports has kept the oil market in check. But the clock is ticking: it's sustaining the flow using a stockpile. And unlike the printing presses of the Fed, it can’t just be topped up with a fresh sheaf of paper.

items.description_html:
On the eve of the Trump-Xi summit, some interesting activity in the Strait of Hormuz, with a Chinese-linked oil tanker making the crossing (via the Iranian new lanes).

If the Yuan Hua Hu VLCC completes the crossing (she has gone dark now), it would be the 3rd Chinese oil tanker to make it through since the war started. The tanker carries 2m barrels of Iraqi oil.

items.title: RT Javier Blas: As the paper oil market rallies, in the North Sea physical market we have seen today the first trade at a **discount** to the Dated Br...
items.description_html:
RT Javier Blas
As the paper oil market rallies, in the North Sea physical market we have seen today the first trade at a **discount** to the Dated Brent benchmark since the war started.

The ironies of the oil market: when plenty (next few days) and dearth (next few weeks) coexist.

items.title: In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY. Exports to China ...
items.description_html:
In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY.

Exports to China fell for the 11th consecutive month, with China’s share of U.S. exports dropping to 3.8%. Exports to Taiwan and India rose YoY.

items.description_html: December’s pickup followed a 7.2% expansion in November, a clear improvement over the 3.7% growth a year earlier. The acceleration was supported by strength in manufacturing, mining, and electricity. Consumer durables and infrastructure goods also posted double-digit gains.

items.title: Americans are very worried about their jobs: The perceived probability among US consumers that the unemployment rate will be higher in 1-year increase...
items.description_html:
Americans are very worried about their jobs:

The perceived probability among US consumers that the unemployment rate will be higher in 1-year increased +0.4 percentage points in April, to 43.9%, the highest in 12 months.

Excluding April 2025, this is the highest reading since the 2020 pandemic spike, according to the NY Fed.

Since the start of 2025, this percentage has risen +10 points.

Meanwhile, 61% of consumers expect unemployment to increase over the next 12 months, according to the University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers.

By comparison, only 14% of respondents expected unemployment to rise in 2021.

This level of pessimism has never happened outside of a recession.

Job market expectations are at recession-like levels.

items.title: BREAKING: US data center construction spending jumped +34% YoY in March, to a record $50 billion annualized rate. Spending on data centers is up +437%...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: US data center construction spending jumped +34% YoY in March, to a record $50 billion annualized rate.

Spending on data centers is up +437% since the beginning of 2021, when the annualized rate stood at ~$9 billion.

This is also up +688% since the start of 2018, when the annualized rate was just ~$6 billion.

Meanwhile, office building construction spending fell -9% YoY in March, to $46 billion, the lowest since 2015.

This means that spending on data centers now exceeds office building construction by $4 billion, or +9%.

To put this into perspective, office construction spending exceeded data center spending by $65 billion, or +650%, in 2020.

AI is fundamentally transforming the US economy.

items.title: Money supply is skyrocketing: Global money supply is now up to a record $121.9 trillion. Over the last 2 years, money supply has soared +$17.1 trillio...
items.description_html:
Money supply is skyrocketing:

Global money supply is now up to a record $121.9 trillion.

Over the last 2 years, money supply has soared +$17.1 trillion, or +16%.

This also marks a +$27 trillion increase, or +28%, since the 2022 low.

This means that global money supply is surging +7% to +8% a year.

Meanwhile, US M2 money supply jumped +$1 trillion YoY, or +4.6%, to a record $22.7 trillion.

Money supply growth is accelerating.

items.title: Risk appetite is skyrocketing: Assets under management (AUM) in US leveraged ETFs are up to a record $177 billion. Since the March bottom, total lever...
items.description_html:
Risk appetite is skyrocketing:

Assets under management (AUM) in US leveraged ETFs are up to a record $177 billion.

Since the March bottom, total leveraged ETF AUM has surged +$45 billion.

Tech-oriented ETFs account for the majority of total AUM, at ~69%

This comes as technology AUM stands at $65 billion, followed by semiconductors at $32 billion, and the Magnificent 7 at $25 billion.

Furthermore, leveraged ETF AUM linked to the S&P 500 is $24 billion.

To put this into perspective, total leveraged ETF AUM was just ~$30 billion in 2020.

Investors are piling into leveraged funds at a record pace.

items.title: BREAKING: US household debt jumped +$18 billion in Q1 2026, to a record $18.8 trillion. Total household debt has surged +$4.6 trillion since January 2...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: US household debt jumped +$18 billion in Q1 2026, to a record $18.8 trillion.

Total household debt has surged +$4.6 trillion since January 2020.

The increase was driven by mortgage debt, which rose +$21 billion, to a record $13.2 trillion.

Auto loans also jumped +$18 billion, to $1.7 trillion, also an all-time high.

Meanwhile, credit card debt declined -$25 billion, to $1.3 trillion, the 2nd-highest on record.

Furthermore, student loan balances fell -$6 billion, to $1.7 trillion, also the 2nd-highest on record.

This comes as delinquency rates on credit card debt and student loans increased to levels last seen near the post-2008 Financial Crisis peaks, weighing on the ability of consumers to take on additional debt.

How much more debt can American households take?

items.title: RT Neelkanth Mishra: With China's significant inventory releases calming physical oil markets (physical premiums are down) but pushing up future oil p...
items.description_html:
RT Neelkanth Mishra
With China's significant inventory releases calming physical oil markets (physical premiums are down) but pushing up future oil prices (Mar-2027 delivery now reaching $85-90), the economy now needs to adjust to sustained higher oil prices.
Of the 2% of GDP terms-of-trade shock, we estimate the government has absorbed around 1.2% so far, and 0.8% has flowed through. As fuel prices at the pump increase, more of the growth headwinds will show up. But the binding constraint is on the external account (the INR), and the economy needs to adjust to energy market realities.
My column today in the Times of India
https://tessellatum.in/?p=619

items.title: RT Maheshwer Peri: It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the succe...
items.description_html:
RT Maheshwer Peri
It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the success rate is 6X of national average. A similar thing happened in 2024 too, but the allegations were brushed under the carpet. Had we dealt with it in 2024 with an iron hand, this wouldnt have repeated.

What we know is that 140 of the 180 questions in NEET were part of a guess paper of 410 questions. What is alarming is that the order of the questions and the options were exactly the same.

The modus operandi is simple. In Sikar, the students are called in for a Mock test, a day before the actual exam and made to prepare for each of the questions in the guess paper. The students had 140 of the 180 questions preepared, thus guaranteed 600 of the 720 marks even before they entered the exam hall.

Time to go after the paper leakers. Jail them. 23 Lac students and families will pay the price of the brushing under the carpet we did in 2024. @careers360

items.title: Lots of countries announced measures early on to deal with the shortages/high prices due to #IranWar. India had earlier invoked the Essential Commodit...
items.description_html:
Lots of countries announced measures early on to deal with the shortages/high prices due to #IranWar.

India had earlier invoked the Essential Commodities Act to protect & control low LPG supply.

A (non-exhaustive) list of what others have done,

1. Philippines: Declared a state of national emergency due to the imminent danger of critically low energy supply.

2. Bangladesh: Daily fuel purchase limits by vehicle type. For eg - bikes up to 2 litres, pvt cars 10 litres etc. Shorter govt office hours. Early closure of markets & malls. And more.

3. S. Korea: Tightened public-sector vehicle rotation from five days to two days; vehicles restricted every alternate business day due to low fuel supply.

4. Indonesia: Announced fuel rationing; mandated civil servants to work from home once a week amid fuel supply disruptions and petrol-station shortages.

5. Australia: Sought agreements with Asian trade partners to secure refined fuel in return for LNG, as hundreds of service stations ran out of fuel.

6. Slovenia: Set a daily cap on road-fuel purchases at gas stations due to shortages at the pump.

#Nifty #GIFTNIFTY #BankNifty #India #IranWar‌ #Oil #Gold

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Macquarie on SBI O-P, TP Rs 1150 4Q FY26: Earnings miss driven by weak margins and trading losses NIM impacted by higher proportion of...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Macquarie on SBI
O-P, TP Rs 1150
4Q FY26: Earnings miss driven by weak margins and trading losses
NIM impacted by higher proportion of floating book
Asset quality remains a bright spot
Stock trades at 1.6x FY27E P/BV, which appears comfortable given expected ROEs of 15%.

JPM on SBI
OW, TP 1225
SBI reported a weaker than expected 4Q with NII (+3.8% y/y, -2% q/q) at Rs 443.8bn moderating vs +9% y/y in 3Q26 and coming 6% below JPM.
SBI recorded a 17bps q/q decline in NIM (whole bank) to 2.81% and an 18bps q/q decline in NIM (domestic) to 2.93%, vs. Street expectations of flattish to marginally lower margins q/q. However, SBI remains confident of NIM recovery in FY27 vs 4Q backed by multiple steps highlighted below.
Asset quality was benign with credit costs (on avg loans) at 24bps (-16bps q/q), although slippages (+3bps q/q) edged up slightly on seasonality.

MS on SBI
EW, TP Rs 980
Focus on call was on sharp NIM decline.
Management expects some recovery in NIM from 4Q level driven largely by loan yield recovery
Assume some recovery, yet cut F27- 28 NIM forecast by >20bp
Trim F27 & F28 EPS forecasts by 4% & 2%
Management guided to FY27 loan growth of 13-15%, assuming no material macro deterioration

CLSA on SBI
O-P TP Rs 1275
Reported a 4% beat on its 4QFY26 PBT driven by lower-than-expected opex and credit costs & a higher recovery from written-off accounts.
However, on top line, it was a weak quarter, with NII missing estimates by 5%.
This was driven by a 17bp sequential compression in NIM, sharper than expected
Calculated loan yields dropped 40bp to 7.5% driven by repo rate cuts, MCLR repricing & an increase in EBLR share.
Apart from NIM, it was a good quarter.
Loan growth of 17% YoY was best in class, while deposit growth picked up from 9% to 11% YoY
CASA ratio was steady YoY at 39%.
Slippages were steady at 0.5%, & credit costs were 0.2%.

BoFA Sec on SBI
Buy, TP Rs 1200
PAT growth of 6% YoY led by strong loan growth and lower credit cost offset by NIM compression and lower non‑interest income
NIM contraction led by repo‑linked loan repricing, MCLR resets post rate cuts, and mix shift towards T‑bill‑linked loans
FY27 guidance - 13-15% loan growth, full year NIM >3% , <50 bps credit cost and 1%+ RoA

Bernstein on SBI
Market Perform, TP Rs 1300
SBI delivered a mixed 4Q26
Loan growth accelerated to 17% YoY but NIM saw a sharp 18 bps QoQ decline to 2.93%, steepest compression amongst large banks.
Slippages rose sequentially, though largely seasonal, while headline GNPA improved to 1.49%.
RoA moderated 12 bps QoQ to 1.07%, weighed down by NIM compression & a Treasury MTM loss.
Management retained FY27 guidance of 13–15% credit growth, >3% domestic NIM & 50 bps credit costs
Management reaffirmed its through-cycle commitment to 1%+ RoA & 15%+ RoE

UBS on SBI
Neutral, TP Rs 1080
NII declined QoQ as margins dip 18bp QoQ due to loan pricing cut
Loan growth at c5% QoQ led by Corporates; asset quality stable
Management guides for loan growth of 13-15% for FY27; credit cost of c50bp
Expect bank's ROA to see downward pressure as treasury gains and credit costs normalize while margin trends remain subdued
Thus estimate ROA of 0.95% over FY27-28E (vs c1.12% in FY26) & believe risk reward at current valuations (1.2x FY27E P/BV) seems fair
Kotak Inst Eqt on SBI

Buy, TP Rs 1250
SBI reported about 5% yoy earnings growth, led by a 55% yoy decline in provisions, while operating profits declined about 10% yoy
Bank reported solid loan growth (~17% yoy), low credit costs (~25 bps) and a NIM decline of 20 bps qoq
Miss against expectations is likely to prompt a near-term pause in its re-rating, with investors awaiting evidence of recovery before rerating bank.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh

items.title: One thing I have seen is people get too lost trying to explore niche themes in the name of being contrarian and then get stuck in bad themes that do n...
items.description_html:
One thing I have seen is people get too lost trying to explore niche themes in the name of being contrarian and then get stuck in bad themes that do not perform even when market does well , do not loose the plot , you want to capture rate of change and valuation safety , you can find it in large , mid and small everywhere if you know how to look , be an opportunist with practicality to identify value and reject ideas that dont

Unnecessary going into <500 crore market cap and seeing some themes , I think not very rewarding exercise

Few people I know can do this well , they can time this microcap liquidity cycle as well as the earnings cycle, and I know its difficult for me , it requires a different lens altogether on evaluating such small companies

items.title: A Masterclass on Cash Flow Statements🚨 Recently reviewed an SME company’s Cash Flow Statement and found some shocking classifications: • Non-curr...
items.description_html:
A Masterclass on Cash Flow Statements🚨

Recently reviewed an SME company’s Cash Flow Statement and found some shocking classifications:

• Non-current liabilities adjusted under working capital changes- even though they belong to Financing Activity/(else depend on line item but not shown as part of Operating Activity)

• Current investments deducted from Operating Cash Flow: turning positive CFO into negative -
Either shown as a part of Investing Activity or as a part of Cash Equivalent

• Only gain/loss on sale of assets shown, but actual sale proceeds missing from Investing Activity

• For the first time ever found interesting line item in Cash Flow from Financing Activity - “Proceeds from issue of Security Premium” 👀(Just Disclosure Change - Proceeds from issue of equity share)

Interestingly How this Man matched the Cash Balance?
Comment below - what can be the possible balancing figure here?

One more Interesting part?
The company raised funds to purchase a corporate office. This is exactly why smart investors don’t stop at Revenue, EBITDA or PAT.

The real story hides inside:
• Cash flow classifications
• Notes to accounts
• Working capital adjustments

#redflags

items.title: Commodity costs were rising sharply, but Ather still improved margins. EBITDA moved from -23.3% to -2.5% over two years (~2080 bps YoY improvement), e...
items.description_html:
Commodity costs were rising sharply, but Ather still improved margins.

EBITDA moved from -23.3% to -2.5% over two years (~2080 bps YoY improvement), even as:
– Aluminium & metals prices rose ~41%
– Battery and electronics costs remained volatile

What helped:
• Better sourcing & inventory planning
• Use of alternate materials (light rare-earth magnets)
• Shift to LFP batteries
• Product design changes

A good example of how execution can offset cost pressures.

items.title: Happy for everyone who made money here in MTAR But fundamentally, valuations are now off the charts In the short term, valuations doesn't matter much ...
items.description_html:
Happy for everyone who made money here in MTAR

But fundamentally, valuations are now off the charts

In the short term, valuations doesn't matter much when momentum is strong

But over the long term, markets do take note of valuations

We have already seen multiple eg where expensive stocks kept moving for some time, but once growth expectations cooled off, the correction was brutal

At high valuations, even a small disappointment in numbers, margins or guidance can lead to sharp derating

Trailing such positions is the better approach

Otherwise, history has shown that the end result in highly expensive stocks is usually not good when expectations become unrealistic


Equity Insights Elite: Lot of discussion around MTAR

Stock is already up 300%

Results are good, but expectations were much higher

Everything aside, current MCap is 20k Cr

This year ended with slightly less than 100 Cr PAT

Even if it does 400-500 Cr PAT, it would still trade at 40-50x

items.description_html:
Nuvama Wealth : 5 Yr OP Profit CAGR : 39%

Dividend of 14 per share was declared for H2FY26🔥

Dividend payout continues to remain at 50% of profit

The key highlight is the changing business mix

Q4 REV grew 7% YoY, but excluding IE & IB, REV grew 13%

FY26 ex IE/IB REV grew 17%, while Wealth + Private grew 20%

Wealth + Private now contributes around 55%

This mix shift is important because these businesses are more predictable compared to Institutional Equities & Investment Banking

Asset Services has recovered fully from the Q1 disruption

Management highlighted that asset services is more like a market infrastructure business, not like a pure capital market cyclical business

The mix is shifting towards Wealth, Private Wealth, Asset Management & Asset Services

This improves predictability & reduces cyclicality

items.title: Just 2 days back, reports said Govt sources had clarified that there were no plans to raise import duties Now, the Govt has increased import duty on g...
items.description_html:
Just 2 days back, reports said Govt sources had clarified that there were no plans to raise import duties

Now, the Govt has increased import duty on gold & silver from 6% to 15%

In 2024, duties were reduced from 15% to 6% to support the jewellery ecosystem, reduce smuggling & improve formalisation.

Now, the same duty has been taken back to 15% to curb imports, reduce pressure on forex reserves & support the rupee.

The intent may be macro stability, but frequent policy reversal creates uncertainty

Existing gold & silver holders, however, would be quietly thanking the government today

items.title: Jewellery stocks were under pressure as markets were sensing that some measures could be taken to curb imports Now, with the Government announcing an ...
items.description_html:
Jewellery stocks were under pressure as markets were sensing that some measures could be taken to curb imports

Now, with the Government announcing an import duty hike, market is factoring in the possibility of some near term demand disruption due to higher gold prices

However, a few things are important to understand.

Jewellery demand in India is largely structural and cultural, driven by weddings, festivals, gifting & long term household buying

Higher duty can impact investment led gold demand more than core jewellery consumption

Historically, duty hikes have led to some moderation in imports, but they have not structurally damaged jewellery demand

Organised retailers are relatively better placed in such an environment because of brand trust, compliance, inventory management etc

A few trends can also play out from here.

Old gold exchange may increase as customers look to recycle existing gold.

Retailers may push more studded jewellery, which usually has better margins.

Valuations across the space have corrected meaningfully from highs, so a large part of the near term concern already seems to be getting factored in

Overall, this looks more like a sentiment impact in the near term

Gold & Silver in MCX up 6% today !

The hard part is not believing in compounding. It is owning a business long enough for the arithmetic to do its work. Most investors sell early because they have no framework for holding.

Terence Tao, aged just 13, got 1/7 on that problem, aced almost everything else, and still won gold.

items.title: RT DepressedBergman: Satyajit Ray on how audience from other countries can connect with his movies: "There are obvious risks in exposing an audience t...
items.description_html:
RT DepressedBergman
Satyajit Ray on how audience from other countries can connect with his movies:

"There are obvious risks in exposing an audience to a wholly alien culture. There was an American lady, for instance, who was so upset by the spectacle of Indians eating with their fingers that she had to leave the theatre as soon as the second dinner episode commenced. But such squeamishness is not common. If details confuse, or offend, or go over the head, there is always the human element to fall back upon.

In 'Apur Sansar' (1959), Apu attends the wedding of a friend’s sister. The bridegroom (this is an arranged marriage) arrives and is found to be touched in the head. With the bride’s mother refusing to give away her daughter, the marriage seems on the verge of being abandoned. If it doesn’t take place at the appointed hour, the bride would be cursed and made unmarriageable for life. The crisis is averted at the last moment by Apu offering to step in as the groom. A Western viewer ignorant of orthodox Hindu customs must find the episode highly bizarre. But since Apu himself finds it so, and since his action is prompted by compassion, the viewer accepts it on moral grounds, though given no opportunity to weigh the pros and cons of a seemingly irrational practice.

Faced with a film from an alien culture, swamped by details which evoke no responses in him, a viewer will often find that the only aspect which offers a common ground is the moral one. Broad concepts of good and evil, right and wrong, do not vary much from culture to culture. Human behaviour also falls within patterns which are largely familiar and predictable. Abnormal situations may affect such behaviour as, say, in Hitler’s Germany; but as long as a film makes the context clear, a viewer is unlikely to lose his bearings. This can happen only when the development of the story itself turns on social and historical factors of which the viewer has no knowledge."

("Deep Focus- Reflections on Cinema", Satyajit Ray, 2011)

P.S: On this day, 67 years ago, 'Apur Sansar' was released in India.

The one silver lining in an otherwise tough macro environment is that the starting point for inflation in India is low.

April CPI came in softer than expected at 3.5%, still below the RBI’s target. It will rise undoubtedly but the low starting point is helpful.

This does give the RBI some wiggle room, if they want to keep rates low. (Not everyone believes that’s a good idea)

Read here: https://www.reuters.com/world/india/india-inflation-edges-up-april-348-energy-risks-grow-2026-05-12/

items.title: RT Tijori: The company is Ajinomoto Co. (TYO: 2802), and the product that matters here is Ajinomoto Build-up Film, or ABF. What makes this interesting...
items.description_html:
RT Tijori
The company is Ajinomoto Co. (TYO: 2802), and the product that matters here is Ajinomoto Build-up Film, or ABF.

What makes this interesting is the switching-cost moat. ABF is a thermosetting film that has to maintain sub-micron flatness across a 600mm sheet, and every single layer needs to be defect-free because one flaw means you scrap the entire 20-layer substrate carrying a $40,000 GPU die. Ajinomoto spent three decades dialing in the exact resin-filler chemistry, controls both the IP and the precursor amino-acid feedstock, and qualifying a new film with TSMC, Intel, or Samsung takes five to seven years of joint testing. So even if Sekisui or a Chinese competitor cracked the chemistry tomorrow, no chipmaker is going to risk a yield disaster just to switch suppliers.

The cleaner play here isn't actually Ajinomoto itself — the food business still accounts for roughly 70% of revenue, which dilutes the AI thesis and is exactly why Palliser has been pushing for a spin-off. If you want pure-play exposure, look downstream at the ABF substrate manufacturers: Unimicron, Nan Ya PCB, Kinsus, Ibiden, Shinko Electric, and AT&S.


SemiAnalysis: There is a single thin-film material required by every AI chip on earth. GPUs, TPUs, custom ASICs. All of them. 98% of global supply controlled by one Japanese chemical company. Zero production-ready alternatives. One producer fully booked through 2027. Raising prices. Lead times

items.title: Tipping the scales: China has caught up. 🇨🇳🇺🇸 In 2000, the US outspent China on R&D by 8x. In 2024, they're tied. 📊 The convergence in ...
items.description_html:
Tipping the scales: China has caught up. 🇨🇳🇺🇸

In 2000, the US outspent China on R&D by 8x. In 2024, they're tied.

📊 The convergence in 4 data points:

GDP: US $30.7T vs China ~$19T — gap shrinking fast on log scale

Productivity: China's output/worker still growing ~5%/yr; US stuck at 0–3%

Exports: China now leads global share in textiles (~35%), electronics (~28%), chemicals (~18%)

R&D spend: 2000: US 36.9% / China 4.6% → 2024: 27.3% / 27.4%

One generation. Total catch-up.

items.title: Why does India trade at a P/B premium? Because it earns one. 15 years of data across 16 markets P/B and ROE move together. India clusters with the US ...
items.description_html:
Why does India trade at a P/B premium?

Because it earns one.

15 years of data across 16 markets P/B and ROE move together. India clusters with the US at the top-right of the curve. Korea, Brazil, and Japan sit bottom-left for the inverse reason.

Multiples reflect return on capital. Always have. The market isn't mispricing India. It's pricing the ROE.

items.title: Pre-Market Update!! From coal to cosmos, paints to ports - India's corporates are spending, winning, and expanding like never before. • Bharti Airtel...
items.description_html:
Pre-Market Update!!

From coal to cosmos, paints to ports - India's corporates are spending, winning, and expanding like never before.

• Bharti Airtel plans to inject ₹20,000 Cr into its NBFC arm, Airtel Money Limited, to bolster digital financial services. Q4 capex stood at ₹16,066 Cr, channelled into 5G, fiber, Connected Homes, Airtel Business, and data centres.

• Cabinet greenlit a ₹37,500 Cr coal gasification initiative aimed at reducing India's dependence on imported fuels, marking a significant push toward domestic energy self-sufficiency and cleaner utilisation of coal reserves.

• India imposed a ban on sugar exports until September 30, 2026, to stabilise domestic prices. The restriction is expected to tighten global supply, buoying international sugar rates while redirecting export opportunities to Brazil and Thailand.

• Lloyds Engineering Works received CCI clearance for its consolidation scheme, absorbing Lloyds Infrastructure & Construction, Metalfab Hitech, and Techno Industries into its fold, streamlining operations under a unified corporate structure.

• Berger Paints undertook two successive price hikes in May- effective May 4 and May 15 aggregating an 11–12% increase, reflecting sustained input cost pressures across its product portfolio.

• Rossel Techsys reported aerospace & defence as its primary growth engine in FY26, backed by robust execution and OEM engagement. Its semiconductor vertical gained traction via qualification wins, while the space segment advanced toward scalable operations.

• Texmaco Rail & Engineering clinched a landmark export contract worth $430.57 Mn (~₹4,045 Cr) from TSIKO Africa Logistics and Barberry Holdings, significantly reinforcing its global footprint in railway infrastructure.

• Department of Financial Services unveiled the Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool with a USD 1.5 Bn corpus and a sovereign guarantee of USD 1.4 Bn (~₹12,980 Cr), ensuring uninterrupted maritime insurance coverage amid escalating Middle East tensions.

• Pasupati Acrylon secured regulatory approval from Uttar Pradesh's Excise Department to scale its ethanol manufacturing capacity from 150 KL/day to 180 KL/day, strengthening its position in India's expanding biofuel ecosystem.

• Nitco bagged an additional marble supply order worth ₹66.65 Cr from Prestige Estates, pushing its cumulative order book from the developer to an impressive ₹347.09 Cr.

• Deccan Gold anticipates a substantial surge in gold output, with management noting that the revised import duty structure will provide a meaningful tailwind to the company's production economics going forward.

• DCM Shriram approved a ₹101 Cr capital outlay for subsidiary Hindusthan Specialty Chemicals, targeting a 36,000 TPA expansion in Formulated Resins capacity, elevating total FR output capability to 50,000 TPA.

• L&T secured significant power transmission & distribution EPC contracts in the Middle East, valued between ₹1,000–2,500 Cr, further cementing its international engineering and infrastructure credentials.

• PNC Infratech's joint venture emerged as the lowest bidder for a ₹571.81 Cr EPC contract to construct a Ganga bridge in Uttar Pradesh, adding a strategic infrastructure project to its order pipeline.

• Bajel Projects won a ₹200–300 Cr order for a 400kV transmission line under India's renewable energy corridor programme, reinforcing its expanding presence in the clean energy transmission space.

• Black Box successfully closed the acquisition of Brazil-based 2S Inovações, adding approximately ₹500 Cr in annualised revenue and meaningfully strengthening its Latin American market presence and delivery capabilities.

• Krishna Defence inked a deal to acquire a 46.81% stake in Taharabadkar Solutions, securing right of first refusal and drag-along rights a strategic move deepening its footprint in India's defence ecosystem.

items.title: For the first time in five years, India's apparel imports from Bangladesh have declined. Rs. 57,324 mn (FY25) → Rs. 52,281 mn (FY26). Meanwhile China...
items.description_html:
For the first time in five years, India's apparel imports from Bangladesh have declined.

Rs. 57,324 mn (FY25) → Rs. 52,281 mn (FY26).

Meanwhile China (+41% YoY), Vietnam (+35%) and Sri Lanka (+23%) all picked up share.

A sourcing re-rating worth watching — political risk, duty structure, or both?

items.title: El Niño and Near-Term Climate Outlook A developing El Niño is expected to increase global temperature anomalies, potentially pushing them to 1.8–2....
items.description_html:
El Niño and Near-Term Climate Outlook

A developing El Niño is expected to increase global temperature anomalies, potentially pushing them to 1.8–2.0°C above pre-industrial levels in the first half of 2027.

The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) estimates a 61% probability of El Niño emerging between May and July 2026, with a ~25% chance of a strong event that could add 0.2–0.3°C to global temperatures.

Subsurface Pacific data suggest El Niño development is progressing faster than expected and likely to persist through the end of 2026.

items.title: KAYNES delivered strong revenue growth of 33.23% YoY in FY26 but missed its Rs. 4,000 Cr revenue guidance by 9.3%. The company did beat EBITDA margin ...
items.description_html:
KAYNES delivered strong revenue growth of 33.23% YoY in FY26 but missed its Rs. 4,000 Cr revenue guidance by 9.3%. The company did beat EBITDA margin guidance (15.76% vs 15.6% target) but faces significant working capital stress with receivables up 166% YoY and operating cash flow deeply negative at Rs. 6,004 Cr.

The ODM segment expansion to 27% of revenue and recent acquisitions provide potential growth drivers, but execution on OSAT capacity and FY28 targets remains challenging.

Positive Factors

FY26 revenue grew 33.23% YoY to Rs. 3,626.35 Cr

FY26 EBITDA margin of 15.76% beat guidance of 15.6% by 16 bps

Q4 revenue rebounded 54.55% QoQ

EBITDA margin recovered 106 bps QoQ from Q3

ODM & Prod. Eng. segment expanded from 10% to 27% of revenue mix

Equity doubled to Rs. 47,625 Cr from QIP proceeds

Acquired 76% stake in Aerocaliph and Cryo Precision in Q4

Risk Factors

FY26 revenue missed guidance of Rs. 4,000 Cr by 9.3%

Operating cash flow deeply negative at Rs. 6,004 Cr (OCF/PAT -1.65x)

Trade receivables grew 165.8% YoY vs revenue 33.2%

PAT declined 21.5% YoY in Q4 due to higher tax and base effect

Total expenses grew faster than revenue (32.6% vs 26.2% YoY)

OSAT plant delayed by 6 months vs target

FY28 revenue target of Rs. 8,500 Cr requires 53% CAGR - execution risk

items.title: Bharti Hexacom delivered a strong Q4 with 5.4% YoY revenue growth and 129 bps EBITDAaL margin expansion. The balance sheet transformation is notable ...
items.description_html:
Bharti Hexacom delivered a strong Q4 with 5.4% YoY revenue growth and 129 bps EBITDAaL margin expansion. The balance sheet transformation is notable — Net Debt/EBITDAaL improved to 0.44x from 0.87x YoY, representing 45% debt reduction.

Homes segment is emerging as a new growth driver with 65% YoY revenue growth and doubling of customer base. Mobile ARPU now at ₹252 exceeds Bharti Airtel's national ARPU, indicating successful premiumization. PAT declined 4.6% YoY due to one-time exceptional charge but PAT before exceptional grew 22.5% YoY.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 5.4% YoY to ₹24,137 Mn (₹2,414 Cr)

EBITDAaL margin expanded 129 bps YoY to 47.9%

Net debt reduced 45% YoY to ₹20,301 Cr, Net Debt/EBITDAaL at 0.44x

Homes segment revenue surged 65.3% YoY to ₹1,172 Cr, customer base doubled to 843K

Mobile ARPU rose to ₹252 from ₹242, now premium to Bharti Airtel national ARPU

Operating FCF grew 22.4% YoY to ₹3,548 Cr

CFO/PAT ratio strong at 2.58x indicating high earnings quality

Mobile customer base grew to 28.77 Mn with 370K net additions

Risk Factors

PAT declined 4.6% YoY to ₹4,467 Mn due to exceptional charge of ₹246 Mn on government levies

Other expenses surged 59.5% YoY — requires monitoring for sustainability

Capex increased 3.3% YoY to ₹15,210 Cr vs guidance of 'marginally unwinding'

Homes segment EBIT at near break-even (₹3 Mn) due to aggressive capex of ₹1,546 Cr

items.title: RT Exencial Research Partners: Solar & ESS policy doesn't stand still — it evolves with renewable penetration. A clean 3-stage framework: Stage 1: So...
items.description_html:
RT Exencial Research Partners
Solar & ESS policy doesn't stand still — it evolves with renewable penetration.

A clean 3-stage framework:

Stage 1: Solar subsidies, ITC, VAT exemption, net metering, FiT. ESS incentives: NIL.

Stage 2: Solar subsidies phase out. Net metering → net billing. ESS gets capacity payments, ITC, VAT exemption.

Stage 3: Solar incentives gone. ESS incentives tied to participation in ancillary services & VPP.

The beneficiary shifts at each stage — equipment makers in early stages, operators & software in mature ones.

India = Stage 1 today. The policy pivot to Stage 2 is the inflection point to track.

items.title: Urban Company has stepped up incentives for both consumers and home service partners in its InstaHelp business during the January–March quarter - the...
items.description_html:
Urban Company has stepped up incentives for both consumers and home service partners in its InstaHelp business during the January–March quarter - the biggest contributor to its losses.

Loss per order increased by 17% to Rs 447, even as net order value declined.

Total losses from InstaHelp nearly doubled to Rs 119 crore, while orders grew 66% to 2.7 million in the January–March quarter compared to October–December.

It will be interesting to see how retention metrics evolve in this category. Some stats for InstaHelp:

- Monthly user retention is 30–33%, with users placing around 2.5 orders per month

- The top 10% of monthly users placed around 8 orders, while the top 1% placed around 21 orders in March 2026

items.title: Chinese goods, which accounted for nearly one-quarter of all US imports before Trump’s first trade war, made up just 7.5% of them in the first quarte...
items.description_html:
Chinese goods, which accounted for nearly one-quarter of all US imports before Trump’s first trade war, made up just 7.5% of them in the first quarter of this year, research from @ChadBown shows.
https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2026/05/12/trump-xi-summit-economy/

items.description_html:
Tariffs are clearly not central to the story of declining fentanyl overdose death. Variations in tariff levels in 2025–26 cannot cause a decline in overdose deaths that began in 2023.

items.title: RT Chad P. Bown: In the run-up to Trump-Xi, my latest look at what’s hidden in the US-China trade data.
items.description_html:
RT Chad P. Bown
In the run-up to Trump-Xi, my latest look at what’s hidden in the US-China trade data.


Peterson Institute: US president Trump & Chinese president Xi are meeting this week.
@ChadBown explains where the trade data between the two countries stand:
